official_name: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
notes: India's Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology — administers the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) and related digital regulations.

data.description: India's primary data protection statute (Act No. 22 of 2023).

data.official_name: Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial and Other Subsidies, Benefits and Services) Act, 2016
data.description: Indian statute creating UIDAI as a statutory authority and authorizing the Aadhaar biometric identity programme. Establishes the legal basis for Aadhaar issuance, authentication, and use by public and private entities.
data.notes: Foundational law for the world's largest biometric identity system. Significantly amended by the Aadhaar and Other Laws (Amendment) Act, 2019 in response to Supreme Court rulings on permissible private-sector use.

data.description: Indian foundational cyber law covering electronic commerce, digital signatures, computer-related offences, and intermediary liability. Significantly amended in 2008 (Section 43A on data protection, Section 79 intermediary safe-harbour, Sections 66/67 cyber offences).
data.notes: Operationalised via the IT Rules 2011 (SPDI Rules), IT Rules 2021 (intermediary guidelines), and CERT-In incident reporting directives.

data.official_name: Information Technology (Reasonable Security Practices and Procedures and Sensitive Personal Data or Information) Rules, 2011
data.description: Subordinate rules under IT Act 2000 Section 43A specifying reasonable security practices for protection of sensitive personal data.
data.notes: Recognises ISO/IEC 27001 as one of the standards meeting 'reasonable security practices'. Largely superseded in scope by the DPDP Act 2023 but still relevant for pre-DPDP cases.

data.official_name: Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules, 2021
data.description: Rules governing intermediaries (social media, messaging platforms) including grievance officer requirements, traceability for significant social media intermediaries, and content moderation obligations.
data.notes: Amended multiple times since 2021; significant amendments in 2022 (online gaming) and 2023 (fact-checking).

data.description: Implementing rules for the DPDP Act (secid:regulation/meity.gov.in/dpdp), notified in the Gazette of India November 13-14, 2025. Operationalizes itemized notices, breach notification, and DPO/audit obligations for Significant Data Fiduciaries.
data.notes: Most substantive provisions phase in over 18 months from notification (~May 2027).

data.description: India's first comprehensive cybersecurity policy framework — critical-infrastructure protection, capability building, and public-private collaboration. Published by the Department of Electronics and IT (now MeitY), effective July 2, 2013.
data.notes: A newer 'National Cyber Security Strategy' has reportedly been drafted by the National Security Council Secretariat but was not formally released as of this sweep entry — revisit.
